124295.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen values participation and communication. With a referendum, there is a risk that residents quickly make decisions based on feelings without knowing all the facts.

124296.explanation: By working together with the municipality to see which matters can be taken over, we contribute to livability and the right to challenge.

124298.explanation: By working closely with the owner and/or initiator to see what is possible at the specific location, we can move more towards tailor-made solutions.

124302.explanation: To achieve our climate goals, green our neighborhoods, and reduce heat stress, we must continue to invest in greenery.

124305.explanation: Not by the municipality alone. Various initiatives from our community want to jointly support this, and we can assist as a municipality.

124308.explanation: As a municipality, we are responsible for the accessibility of our own buildings. TOP/Gemeentebelangen sees no financial role for the municipality in this regarding entrepreneurs. This is their own responsibility.

124309.explanation: By encouraging developments in cooperation with the owner, municipality, and province, you can enable planning.

124310.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to remove barriers for families with a tight budget and give extra attention to people with a disability. By handling subsidies differently, we can make sports clubs more accessible to all residents.

124312.explanation: The municipality supports the youth sports and culture fund, the cultural menus, and cultural education, which make it possible for children to engage with music. Also, by supporting Toonbeeld, TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to contribute to making music education possible.

124313.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen values looking at subsidies in a new way, making associations future-proof. Also, by focusing on cooperation and merging, more can be achieved and associations maintained.

124315.explanation: Many tasks have been added from the police, and enforcement is closely watched by society. TOP/Gemeentebelangen values investing in sufficient enforcement officers.

124316.explanation: This is part of the broad safety policy. According to TOP/Gemeentebelangen, camera surveillance should not be an end in itself but must be part of the whole.

124318.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to further stimulate housing construction for all target groups, including the elderly.

124319.explanation: By focusing on building homes for all target groups, TOP/Gemeentebelangen aims to ensure quick transitions and permanent residences for all inhabitants.

124320.explanation: By focusing on infill development within existing cores, we can add homes, but by allowing large-scale housing construction on the edges outside built-up areas, building needs can be accelerated.

124323.explanation: By focusing within current agreements, we can help more people out of poverty and deploy resources more efficiently.

124325.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen strives for a good balance between different parking spaces. In existing residential neighborhoods, this is difficult. Together with residents and businesses, we seek smart solutions to park all vehicles, including electric ones. Among other things, through the use of smart charging stations.

124326.explanation: Regarding traffic safety within the built-up area, TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to maintain the current policy, except for main roads through the municipality.

124327.explanation: By providing a diverse range of caregivers, all residents in need can be served. The condition must be that there are no unacceptable cost increases for users or the municipality.

124329.explanation: The principle of TOP/Gemeentebelangen is and remains to keep rates low and only adjust them with inflation correction.

124330.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to work towards abolishing the dog tax, provided that coverage can be found in the budget. As long as the national government views this tax as a legitimate tax instrument, coverage must be provided. If the government abolishes it, they must find coverage.

124334.explanation: As long as national legislation has not changed, TOP/Gemeentebelangen strives to comply with the law.

124336.explanation: By focusing on the use of existing roofs, a large part of the energy generation task can be achieved.

124338.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to work, together with the sector, to make it possible to retain the cinema. If it disappears, there will be no cinema left in Terneuzen.

124341.explanation: According to current agreements, there may only be one coffee shop in Terneuzen. TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to maintain this.

124342.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to maintain the current 30% social rental housing in new construction plans.

124344.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen stands for good, quality housing for all target groups. By adhering to current rules, we can stimulate this.

124345.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to maintain the current level of facilities in all centers as it is now and continue investing in them.

124346.explanation: Terneuzen is one of the cheapest municipalities in the Netherlands for parking. Due to choices made a few years ago regarding rates, we see more visitors coming.

124347.explanation: TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to work together with residents and the municipality to find smart solutions in existing neighborhoods to better balance parking and livability. Climate adaptation, water storage, greening, and heat stress are important issues to consider.

124348.explanation: Several streets in the center are becoming car-free. TOP/Gemeentebelangen strives for a better balance between cars, cyclists, and pedestrians. Cars remain welcome in the city center, but not everywhere.

124349.explanation: Besides a sporting function, swimming pools also have a meeting function. Our residents deserve to keep the pools. By creative yet adequate use of available possibilities and through collaboration with local entrepreneurs, these facilities can continue to exist.

124351.explanation: The realization of Campus Terneuzen offers benefits for the entire region. We build a better educational climate but also the economic and social future of our region. This area will become a vibrant place for education in the broadest sense.

124352.explanation: By working together with residents to see what possibilities we can find, TOP/Gemeentebelangen wants to invest in more locations for off-leash dogs.

124353.explanation: As a municipality, we have no authority in the discussion of whether or not to have a nuclear power plant. TOP/Gemeentebelangen supports the inventory and focus on a high-quality package of conditions in consultation with residents and entrepreneurs by listening to their concerns and conveying them.

124295.explanation: The CDA only finds the use of a consultative referendum useful for local issues.

124296.explanation: This fits with our aim to stimulate volunteer work and citizen involvement.

124298.explanation: Such a development fits with the CDA’s commitment to preserving facilities and livability. Ideally, this involves developing branch-related businesses.

124302.explanation: This aligns with our concern for a good climate and healthy living environment.

124305.explanation: The CDA supports a festive New Year's Eve and opposing illegal fireworks and all their consequences.

124308.explanation: The CDA does not see this as a government task. Accessibility goes hand in hand with good business management.

124309.explanation: This strongly depends on the situation and location. The CDA promotes livability in rural areas but within the boundaries of the Environmental Vision.

124310.explanation: This is existing policy and is supported by the CDA. We already contribute to this through vouchers and sports funds.

124312.explanation: The CDA supports promoting art and culture and therefore refers to funds like those from the Stichting Leergeld Zeeuws-Vlaanderen.

124313.explanation: As CDA, we believe the municipality should help think of solutions without this always involving financial contributions. Think of solutions such as bundling volunteers, multifunctional use, reimbursement of framework training, etc.

124315.explanation: The CDA endorses the concept of enforcement by public order officers (boa’s) instead of police deployment for many of these matters. There is a structural shortage of boa’s. That is why the municipality of Terneuzen continuously calls for people to work as boa’s in our municipality.

124316.explanation: The Netherlands is behind in installing camera surveillance. This makes it difficult to perform a proper analysis in times of societal threats. The use of collected images should take place in consultation with responsible government agencies.

124318.explanation: For the CDA, this applies to all centers and makes it possible for elderly and young people to continue living in their own community.

124319.explanation: For the CDA, the quality of housing is important. Homes must meet requirements for permanent residence. If vacation homes are not equipped accordingly, permanent residence is undesirable. Furthermore, it must be prevented that exploitative renting arises for housing, for example, of migrant workers.

124320.explanation: CDA wants to build more houses and develop housing. Our aim is to expand within the existing boundaries of the built-up area, for example through demolition and replacement new construction of (vacant) old buildings and houses.

124323.explanation: The CDA generally strives for issue-related customization, for example in the energy cost allowance: from 120% to 130% of the minimum.

124325.explanation: For the CDA, this is a matter of supply and demand.

124326.explanation: The CDA supports adjusting the maximum speed according to the traffic situation, residential environment, and the associated risks for road users and residents. In general, we should give children, cyclists, and pedestrians more space and safety.

124327.explanation: For the CDA, the municipality facilitates care with overall accessibility.

124329.explanation: The CDA believes that the property tax (OZB) should align with the actual value of the real estate.

124330.explanation: The CDA sees the need to include a recalibration of this tax in the coalition agreement.

124334.explanation: The CDA believes that the municipality must meet its national obligation to provide and allocate housing to status holders.

124336.explanation: The CDA supports placing solar panels on roofs of, for example, warehouses and on land that is unusable due to the presence of pipes, cables, and conduits.

124338.explanation: The CDA supports an existing plan for the Culture Quarter. The advantage is the bundling of forces and financial resources as a driving factor combined with joint management.

124341.explanation: The current situation meets a need.

124342.explanation: The CDA advocates for a good balance in the number of social rental homes compared to owner-occupied homes. Centralized ownership and management of rental homes prevents deterioration.

124344.explanation: In Terneuzen, this can lead to subletting and undesirable situations such as landlord exploitation. Especially labor migrants and local residents will suffer the negative consequences.

124345.explanation: The CDA sees such facilities as a place for affordable gatherings, which helps counter loneliness.

124346.explanation: Rates in Terneuzen are already very low.

124347.explanation: The CDA believes that, based on current car ownership, the existing policy regarding parking spaces according to the current standards is insufficient.

124349.explanation: The CDA strives to maintain the current facilities.

124351.explanation: The CDA sees the Campus as an added value. However, we must keep this ambition affordable.

124352.explanation: As with many facilities in public spaces, clear rules also apply to off-leash areas. This is to protect people and animals.

124353.explanation: The CDA is against building nuclear power plants in the municipality of Terneuzen due to the unwanted impact on the environment.

124295.explanation: We are not in favor of referendums. Consultation through elections and dialogue tables.

124296.explanation: It is important that the municipality takes responsibility for facilities such as a sports hall. This should not be left to the residents. For the maintenance of parks, we see possibilities.

124302.explanation: Agreed. It is also important that we plant various types.

124305.explanation: Agreed, a beautiful show in a central place in the municipality. A great celebration is celebrated together.

124308.explanation: Agreed, we are happy to provide a subsidy.

124309.explanation: The rural area deserves our protection.

124310.explanation: Agreed, we are committed to a sports fund for youth and adults.

124312.explanation: We find accessible music education important.

124313.explanation: We support maintaining (sports) facilities even if it costs extra money.

124316.explanation: Privacy is a great value. It depends on the area.

124318.explanation: We stand for good residential care policy.

124319.explanation: People have the right to a real home.

124320.explanation: There must be room to build under conditions in the rural area.

124323.explanation: We support increasing the threshold from 120% to 130%.

124325.explanation: It is important to have sufficient spaces for electric charging to stimulate sustainable mobility.

124326.explanation: It provides safety and is good for the environment.

124327.explanation: That luxury is not available, moreover, we believe that religion should not play a role in the healthcare landscape.

124329.explanation: It is a nice contribution to the budget. We finance facilities from this.

124330.explanation: Revenue invested in facilities for dogs, such as dog walking areas.

124334.explanation: All emergency seekers deserve equal treatment. We pay attention to the most urgent situations for everyone.

124336.explanation: There can be a valid reason for it.

124338.explanation: We focus on preserving the cinema. A combination is conceivable.

124341.explanation: Allowing more coffee shops creates competition. This benefits the quality.

124342.explanation: Currently, only 20% is social housing; this must increase.

124344.explanation: Unless they meet strict requirements and have a permit for room rental. Vulnerable neighborhoods are spared; no room rental is allowed there. We also believe this should only be allowed for permanent residence.

124345.explanation: A strong supply of facilities per village is very important for livability.

124346.explanation: We are already very affordable. In our parking garages, the first 2 hours are free with a maximum of 4 euros per day. Free parking will displace local residents.

124347.explanation: Preferably more greenery and play facilities.

124348.explanation: Car-free zones are fine, but local residents must still be able to park close to home.

124349.explanation: We stand for preserving our swimming pools. A nice facility nearby. Promotes health and community spirit. With the new admission policy, the pools are once again a pleasant and safe place.

124351.explanation: Good education and a well-equipped building are necessary for the future of our youth and thus the region and its economy.

124352.explanation: We support well-equipped dog play areas and also believe that dogs must be leashed within the built-up area.

124353.explanation: We are against nuclear energy. We see much more potential in offshore wind, battery storage, and hydrogen development. If the national government decides to designate our area, we aim for good integration and strict conditions as proposed by the advisory group.

124295.explanation: Referendums can be used to guide important and/or major issues. The VVD believes the municipal council plays an important role in listening to the wishes and concerns from the local community.

124296.explanation: If residents see added value in taking over municipal management, the option must exist.

124298.explanation: The VVD believes entrepreneurs should be free to organize their business as they wish.

124302.explanation: The VVD believes that nature compensation can also be applied in other ways. We support promoting a broad strategy.

124305.explanation: We support organizing a fireworks show in every city, village, or neighborhood.

124308.explanation: The VVD believes this is the entrepreneurs' own responsibility.

124309.explanation: The owner may decide what he does with his property. If the farmer wishes to build on his land, this must be made possible.

124310.explanation: Vitality is very important, but financial health is the foundation for a prosperous life.

124312.explanation: Music lessons must be accessible to all children, so we support including this in the primary school curriculum.

124313.explanation: Under certain conditions, there should be room to provide support. This applies not only to sports clubs but also to other organizations with social importance such as youth associations and cultural institutions.

124315.explanation: The VVD supports more police presence on the streets and thus enforcement.

124316.explanation: Camera surveillance is a serious measure and should be used under certain circumstances, not in general.

124318.explanation: The VVD wants small rental and owner-occupied homes to be built for all layers of society.

124319.explanation: Residents should be able to dispose of their property as they wish.

124320.explanation: Residents should be able to dispose of their property as they wish.

124323.explanation: Poverty must be fought, and the best remedy is a full-fledged job. The municipality must look at how it supports residents living in poverty, providing customized solutions where possible. The threshold for extra support in Terneuzen is already above the social assistance standard.

124325.explanation: There are national guidelines for this. The municipality adheres to them. That is sufficient.

124326.explanation: We are not in favor of a generic scheme.

124327.explanation: The VVD supports freedom of choice. Incidentally, this often falls outside municipal authority.

124329.explanation: It would be nice if that were possible, but given the expected negative development of the municipal fund (money flowing from the national government to municipalities), it is not realistic. Any increase in property tax must be limited to inflation at most.

124330.explanation: Dog tax belongs to the general funds. It is not intended to be used for the benefit of dogs and their owners. Just as property tax is not intended to be used for the benefit of houses and their owners. If one is abolished, the other must increase.

124334.explanation: It must be looked at who needs it; this requires customized work from the municipality regarding individual cases.

124336.explanation: We are not against solar panels on agricultural land. The VVD believes that fields with solar panels are a misuse of strategic agricultural land.

124338.explanation: The VVD believes cultural centers in our municipality should be better utilized before investing in new locations.

124341.explanation: Under certain conditions, this may be permitted.

124342.explanation: The percentage of social rental housing in the Netherlands is the highest in Western and Northern Europe. Increasing this further makes the housing market more difficult and helps no one.

124344.explanation: The VVD believes every landlord may decide this for themselves. However, the existing regulations must be met and social cohesion must not be jeopardized.

124345.explanation: Village centers are important for local cohesion within a village. They are places where residents can come together and associations can exist. We believe that every larger village should make use of a village center if this is financially feasible.

124346.explanation: We advocate for as little paid parking as possible in our municipality. Paid parking hinders accessibility and activities.

124347.explanation: We also encourage the dual use of private parking lots and parking garages. This space is also important for entrepreneurs.

124348.explanation: That would be disastrous for virtually all activities and developments in the center.

124349.explanation: Livability deteriorates sharply if we also close this facility for our residents.

124351.explanation: The municipality has started implementing the housing plans for secondary education, the education campus. So much has already been invested that cancelling this project would be too costly.

124352.explanation: The current facilities are more than sufficient.

124353.explanation: The VVD supports nuclear energy; we see this as an important addition to the energy mix. The municipality must be fully involved from the start to properly manage the process and achieve maximum benefit for all residents.

124295.explanation: Residents should have direct influence over important decisions. Referendums strengthen democracy and ensure that administrators listen better to society.

124296.explanation: Local involvement deserves space, as long as quality and safety are guaranteed.

124298.explanation: Farmers should be given the space to earn extra income and undertake business activities. This strengthens the countryside and the local economy, as long as it fits within the environment and does not cause nuisance.

124302.explanation: Rigid rules do not work. Tree cutting is sometimes necessary for safety, housing construction, or infrastructure. The municipality should provide customized solutions instead of mandatory replanting.

124305.explanation: No fireworks ban; this is a tradition that belongs to the Netherlands, provided the fireworks are responsible and approved. Order and safety come first. Rioters and troublemakers should be punished more severely. Moving large groups of people from the region to a central point poses risks.

124308.explanation: Accessibility is important, but financial support should not automatically fall to the municipality and taxpayer. Entrepreneurs remain responsible themselves; the municipality should think along but not subsidize.

124309.explanation: Farmers should be given more room to voluntarily build on their own land, for example for business succession or family. This strengthens the countryside and prevents unnecessary bureaucracy. The municipality must cooperate in this.

124310.explanation: Sports are important, but it is not the municipality's task to pay membership fees. There are already arrangements (Youth Fund for Sports and Culture and Learning Money Foundation) and associations often provide tailored solutions. Personal responsibility must remain the starting point.

124312.explanation: Music lessons are valuable, but this is not a core task of the municipality. Public money should first go to basic skills in education (writing, reading, arithmetic), safety, and basic facilities. Parents and schools are primarily responsible for extra activities.

124313.explanation: Sports clubs fulfill an important social function. Temporary support may be necessary, but not structural. Clubs should remain as independent as possible and not become dependent on municipal subsidies.

124315.explanation: More visible enforcement increases safety on the streets. The municipality has no control over police capacity but does over enforcement officers who should focus on nuisance, order, and safety, not on patronizing. Those who misbehave must be dealt with immediately.

124316.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent crime and nuisance and facilitates detection. The safety of residents comes first. Cameras are deployed specifically at problem locations.

124318.explanation: There is a great shortage of suitable housing for the elderly. By building more smaller homes, elderly people can move on, and larger family homes remain available. This strengthens livability in neighborhoods and villages.

124319.explanation: In exceptional cases, permanent residence in holiday homes can help solve the housing shortage. This must be done carefully, without nuisance, and with clear agreements about safety and facilities.

124320.explanation: The housing shortage calls for realistic solutions. New construction outside village boundaries must remain possible if there is insufficient space within the villages. The municipality must provide customized solutions and avoid getting stuck in rigid rules. Expropriation of farmland is out of the question.

124323.explanation: People who fall just above the current threshold must not fall between the cracks. Help must be aimed at genuine need and remain affordable. The municipality must provide tailored solutions and prevent abuse.

124325.explanation: Parking spaces are scarce and must remain available for everyone. The municipality should not unilaterally favor electric drivers. The choice for electric driving is up to residents themselves, not the government.

124326.explanation: A general reduction to 30 km/h causes frustration and is not necessary everywhere. Traffic measures must focus on real bottlenecks. The municipality must apply tailored solutions instead of a generic measure.

124327.explanation: Choice in care is important. Care must primarily be professional, accessible, and affordable for everyone but must not include religious or cultural demands that conflict with equality. Men and women are equal, and discrimination against care providers is unacceptable.

124329.explanation: Housing costs are already high enough. The municipality must stop raising taxes and instead reduce property tax. Residents should have more money left in their wallets.

124330.explanation: Dog tax is an unfair and outdated tax. Dog owners receive no extra facilities in return. This tax should be abolished.

124334.explanation: Social rental homes are scarce. Residents of Terneuzen who live and work here should be given priority. It is unacceptable that they wait for years while status holders are prioritized.

124336.explanation: Farmland is intended for food production, not for solar parks. Solar panels belong on roofs and industrial sites. The municipality must protect the countryside from chaos.

124338.explanation: The municipality must be cautious with costly prestige projects. Existing facilities should first be optimally used, and financial risks avoided. Public money should go to core tasks, not new multi-million projects.

124341.explanation: Coffeeshops bring nuisance and crime. Terneuzen does not need extra drug tourism. The municipality should focus on safety and livability, not on expanding coffeeshops.

124342.explanation: Rigid percentages backfire. There is a need for affordable homes in all segments. The municipality must steer on flow and tailor-made solutions, not on national quotas that delay housing construction.

124344.explanation: Room rental often leads to nuisance, parking pressure, and degradation of neighborhoods. Houses are intended for families and starters. The municipality must prevent abuses. Currently, much nuisance is experienced due to labor migrants being crammed in. Occasional rental, for example to a student, should remain possible.

124345.explanation: Village centers strengthen livability and social cohesion. This must remain affordable and match residents' needs. No prestige projects, but functional meeting places.

124346.explanation: High parking rates drive away visitors and customers. Cheaper parking strengthens the center and helps entrepreneurs. Cars must remain accessible for residents and visitors. For Terneuzen, free parking would be best as long as visitor numbers remain low.

124347.explanation: Parking pressure in neighborhoods is high. Residents must be able to park their cars close to home. The municipality must stop removing parking spaces and ensure sufficient capacity.

124348.explanation: A car-free center makes the center less accessible for the elderly, families, and entrepreneurs. Shops must remain easily reachable. Cars are part of Terneuzen's reality.

124349.explanation: Outdoor swimming pools are important for recreation, health, and livability, especially in villages. Closure mainly affects families and the elderly. The municipality must preserve facilities instead of further dismantling them.

124351.explanation: Education is important, but large projects should not become money-draining prestige plans. The municipality must first provide clarity about costs, necessity, and alternatives before taking further steps. Construction of Lodewijk College must start as soon as possible. Split and freeze the plan.

124352.explanation: Dog owners deserve more space, provided this happens safely and without nuisance. Designated off-leash areas are a good solution. It remains necessary to take other users into account.

124353.explanation: Nuclear energy is reliable, clean, and necessary for our energy supply. It provides employment and energy security. Terneuzen can play an important role in this. Preconditions are a must. National energy supply is very important to avoid dependence on foreign countries.

124295.explanation: A referendum is a false form of participation. Important and complex issues cannot be reduced to a simple yes or no. Moreover, it has been shown that referenda are very easy to manipulate.

124296.explanation: Only for larger buildings or projects with a proper continuity arrangement.

124298.explanation: In principle in favor, but 'always' is somewhat unnuanced. Only agricultural products and/or related goods allowed in a shop. Camping is allowed, provided it is limited in size.

124305.explanation: The centers outside Terneuzen get nothing from this. A possible alternative is to allow local (neighborhood) associations to set off fireworks on New Year's Eve under strict conditions.

124310.explanation: Sounds sympathetic, but how will the municipality check that this is demonstrably used?

124312.explanation: Agreed, but this is already happening.

124313.explanation: At most for very specific projects or purposes.

124315.explanation: Sounds good, but enforcement officers are barely available.

124316.explanation: Camera surveillance is effective, but it interferes so heavily with privacy that we wonder whether it outweighs the feeling of insecurity.

124319.explanation: Holiday homes are generally not suitable for permanent residence.

124320.explanation: As long as solutions within the existing village boundaries have not yet been exhausted, yes.

124323.explanation: To 130% of the social assistance standard instead of 120% as it is now.

124326.explanation: Only within residential neighborhoods. On through roads like Rooseveltlaan this is neither necessary nor useful.

124327.explanation: For our party, that is obviously a given.

124329.explanation: The current arrangement may remain in place as far as we are concerned.

124330.explanation: The current arrangement may remain in place as far as we are concerned.

124334.explanation: Priority housing for status holders can only cease if a realistic alternative is offered that prevents clogging of the asylum chain. This alternative is currently lacking.

124336.explanation: Banning goes too far. But expansion of the current number is undesirable, not least for aesthetic reasons.

124338.explanation: Preservation or a restart under new ownership of the cinema facility is preferred. Giving this building a new destination now goes too far.

124341.explanation: We certainly should not encourage this.

124342.explanation: At least 40% sounds good but is practically unfeasible. We strive for 30%, which already seems ambitious to us.

124344.explanation: Yes, the housing shortage is so high that these kinds of unorthodox measures are negotiable as far as we are concerned.

124346.explanation: The current arrangement whereby you park for free the first two hours in parking garages is fine. And even if you park longer, the rate is very reasonable.

124347.explanation: We are not aware of structural parking problems in existing neighborhoods.

124348.explanation: Car-restricted is fine, car-free goes a step too far.

124349.explanation: The current number of five does not need to be maintained at all costs in our opinion.

124351.explanation: The Christian Union has always been critical of the location and was one of the very few to oppose. But ‘the ink is dry’ and reconsideration will cause even more delay.

124353.explanation: Building new nuclear power plants in Zeeland is only justified with very generous compensation. Zeeland must not become a ‘nuclear exploitation area.’ And although we as the municipal council have no authority over this, we speak out against Paulinapolder as a possible location.

124295.explanation: However, surveying residents on genuinely important topics such as the realization of nuclear power plants is a useful tool to gauge how our inhabitants think about it.

124296.explanation: Taking over small matters like managing a public park is fine. A sports hall goes too far; then the municipality remains responsible for maintenance, safety, etc., and that should not be placed on the residents.

124298.explanation: Where possible, we support farmers who (partly) cease their business activities in developing new or additional economic activities.

124302.explanation: We maintain at least the same area of green spaces. We preferably see more greening so that the living environment improves for people and animals.

124305.explanation: No, the money we would spend on that is better returned to our residents.

124308.explanation: This is an obligation for the entrepreneurs themselves.

124309.explanation: Only if that land is converted into a valid zoning plan making residential construction possible.

124310.explanation: Sports are not a basic necessity of life. People can also exercise in other ways, individually or in groups.

124312.explanation: These kinds of extras must be paid for by the parents themselves.

124313.explanation: If sports take place in an association context, a substantial membership fee must also be charged. Self-reliance can also help to get the finances back in order.

124315.explanation: The well-being of the residents is under pressure regarding safety; therefore, it is necessary to increase the number of neighborhood enforcement officers (BOA's).

124316.explanation: Additions only at locations where insecurity is currently experienced.

124318.explanation: Yes, we explicitly take the needs of the elderly into account in new construction plans, such as lifetime-proof homes, good accessibility, and proximity to care facilities.

124319.explanation: Permanent residence in recreational homes is only possible under strict conditions. For example, sufficient facilities must be present and permanent residence must be necessary given the local housing demand.

124320.explanation: Small expansions of villages, like adding a street, promote a stable and viable village community!

124323.explanation: We adhere to the national standard of 120% of the social minimum.

124325.explanation: Many private individuals have their own charging point. There are still enough commercial charging stations in our municipality. The number of electric cars is not increasing substantially.

124326.explanation: Only where necessary at neighborhood level, where child safety is at risk. Not on through roads and ring roads.

124327.explanation: The municipality ensures that the legal right of care recipients to freedom of choice in care is practically guaranteed, so that they receive care that aligns with their beliefs.

124329.explanation: We need these tax revenues to finance various tasks in our municipality.

124330.explanation: For many people a dog is a companion, especially for singles. Having a dog also means that people get more exercise.

124334.explanation: We should treat refugees and natives in the same way; both groups urgently need housing.

124336.explanation: Indeed, it is a bad development to use good agricultural land for solar panels. Environmental quality also plays a major role; the landscape industrializes and loses experiential value. Let's first use the roofs—there is still plenty of space to install panels there.

124338.explanation: Terneuzen already has sufficient cultural institutions.

124341.explanation: Yes, because that only causes undesirable side effects; such as addiction among residents (with all its negative impacts), attracting customers from distant areas, social safety issues, and hygiene problems.

124342.explanation: We adjust the share of social rental homes according to Terneuzen’s needs, not according to national quotas.

124344.explanation: We are already facing strong pressure on the villages due to labor migrants living in residential houses. This must be managed through a permit policy and enforcement. Allowing individual room rentals only exacerbates the problem!

124345.explanation: The larger villages and cores function as they have historically developed. It is not relevant whether there is a center. However, we strive to maintain as many shops as possible in the villages. This is positive for living enjoyment and allows elderly residents to stay in the villages.

124346.explanation: There is currently a good balance. In the parking garages, the first hours can be parked for free. This sends a positive signal to the retailers in the Terneuzen center.

124347.explanation: Parking spaces are made according to a national standard. There are no locations where the number of parking spaces is under pressure. The municipality of Terneuzen complies with this.

124348.explanation: Do not do this because elderly people and people with disabilities must anyway be able to reach the center. It is also disadvantageous for retailers.

124349.explanation: The swimming pools fulfill a need; downsizing is undesirable.

124351.explanation: We must stop spending money without limits. Therefore, we are firmly against building a building for Scalda and the CTT for 40 million and using a rental construction for Scalda and CTT! There are far too many risks for Terneuzen with this.

124352.explanation: Dogs running loose can cause dangerous and undesirable situations.

124353.explanation: For several reasons, Zeeuws-Vlaanderen is not suitable for a nuclear power plant; the location is currently pristine polder, construction would have a massive impact on this small-scale area (15 years of 24/7 construction with 15,000 workers) which is hardly imaginable, high pressure on infrastructure, etc.

124295.explanation: Strive for a Citizens’ Advisory Council.

124296.explanation: Provided the municipality provides financial support.

124309.explanation: Depending on the need and zoning plan. Only for personal use or informal care.

124316.explanation: The municipality must take a proactive approach to this.

124318.explanation: Knarrenhof-style housing in the village centers.

124326.explanation: Except for access/main roads and ribbon developments, unless better connections are established.

124344.explanation: Provided it meets the correct standard.

124347.explanation: Closely monitor where there is a need for parking space or nuisance occurs.

124348.explanation: Residents of the city center must always be able to reach their homes by car for loading and unloading. And park within 100 meters.

124295.explanation: The SP wants more attention to be given to the interests of the people and acted upon. A referendum can be a means for this. It is important, however, to provide very good and objective information about the relevant subject beforehand. Council members and interest groups could also be involved in this.

124296.explanation: The SP favors more control for people in neighborhoods over their own environment. The SP also wants housing corporations to become real associations with self-governance by tenants and tenant participation. For example, 70% of tenants must be in favor of demolishing homes before they are actually torn down.

124298.explanation: A shop selling products from local farms is good for both residents and farmers. The SP is not in favor of campsites on all farms. Given the current housing shortage, a few tiny houses on a farmyard would be a good idea again.

124302.explanation: The SP wants that for every felled tree (often mature after many years), at least two new trees are planted. We already have little greenery here, so it is important to protect our trees and keep the living environment healthy and sustainable.

124305.explanation: By organizing a fireworks show for everyone, social gatherings can be encouraged. The SP supports a form of fireworks enjoyment because the ban on purchasing personal fireworks will come into effect in 2027. Enforcement of the ban remains important, especially in neighborhoods where children and dogs are outside.

124308.explanation: Large companies are expected to allocate their own budgets and set an example. Smaller organizations may approach the municipality. It is important that no one is excluded due to poor physical or communication accessibility.

124309.explanation: There is a housing shortage; the SP supports loosening regulations regarding living on farmland. When it involves renting multiple homes on one piece of farmland, the SP believes at least 50% should be social rental housing, with rent up to 800 euros per month.

124310.explanation: A social assistance benefit is not sufficient for a complete and healthy life. A sports club membership benefits a person. This is an impulse for a healthy and positive life. The municipality may also continue to contribute to music and culture (Toonbeeld).

124312.explanation: Ideally, music lessons should be organized during school hours at school.

124313.explanation: Sports clubs could creatively come up with ways to generate income or save costs, for example by organizing a bingo, participating with Jantje Beton, etc. At the same time, some sports clubs really cannot continue to operate without support.

124315.explanation: More personnel is needed both for the police and municipal enforcement. The municipality must also press the national government for more funding for police forces and enforcement. The profession should be made more attractive, with better remuneration.

124316.explanation: At some larger bus stops, parking garages, and neighborhoods with a lot of nuisance, cameras can contribute to prevention. At the same time, the SP wants to avoid over-securing our public spaces, as this would come at the expense of privacy. The SP wants some fixed cameras at set locations and some flexibly deployable cameras.

124318.explanation: Many more affordable homes must be built, such as apartments and kangaroo houses. Focus on multiple land use. Not only for the elderly, but also for young people, first-time buyers, small families, and movers. Vacancy should also be combated through vacancy taxes.

124319.explanation: As long as there is a housing shortage, solutions-oriented thinking is required. Temporarily allowing permanent residence in recreational homes can be part of this. The livability of all residential neighborhoods must remain the standard. Custom solutions are necessary.

124320.explanation: On a small scale, new construction can take place outside the cores with as good integration as possible. So, it depends on the situation.

124323.explanation: The SP wants to raise this limit to 140% of the social assistance standard.

124325.explanation: Half of the cars sold are electric. Charging stations are often occupied and can also malfunction. A charging point near home or work makes electric driving feasible and more attractive. The municipality may designate these locations.

124326.explanation: The entire built-up area would be too much. In unclear places within the built-up area, speed could be reduced to 30 km/h. Also, good attention should be given to the best traffic flow in the safest way. All neighborhoods should be provided with safe, wide bike paths. This can be done by introducing one-way traffic for motorized vehicles in those streets.

124327.explanation: It should be the other way around: caregivers should always take into account the faith, culture, and beliefs of the patient or client and not discriminate. It is good when people from different cultures and backgrounds come into contact with each other.

124329.explanation: The SP wants to differentiate property tax (OZB) rates as much as possible: keep the property tax for residents the same and increase it for businesses.

124330.explanation: Dog tax is an outdated, obsolete tax. Moreover, it is not fair. But the municipality should ensure sufficient off-leash areas and ensure dog droppings are properly cleaned from parks.

124334.explanation: Many more social rental homes must be built because not only refugees are waiting too long for a home now. Many more affordable homes must be constructed, such as apartments and kangaroo homes. Focus on multiple land use.

124336.explanation: Solar panels on farmland are a way to generate clean energy. Moreover, you can still grow vegetables underneath, let sheep graze, or, for example, park underneath. Much cleaner and cheaper than polluting, dangerous, and expensive nuclear power plants, which also come too late.

124338.explanation: The cinema should just become a cinema again. The SP wants to introduce a 'Passion for Terneuzen' card. With this subscription, you can participate affordably in various cultural, sports, and recreational activities. A special rate of 5 euros per address applies for people with a low income.

124341.explanation: Competition is no problem as long as the rules are followed.

124342.explanation: There is really a huge need for more social rental homes! Previously, people had to wait a year; now almost five years. If this problem is solved, positive effects on other policy areas will follow immediately!

124344.explanation: Room rental could contribute to solving the housing shortage, but the nature of the rental contract must be carefully considered. It should aim to provide housing to family members or students and not be used as a commercial model to exploit more labor migrants.

124345.explanation: A cultural village center, with its own community center, a subsidized neighborhood shop (without rent), a place for the village youth, and also for the elderly is urgently needed for social cohesion. Residents can themselves (co)shape its content.

124346.explanation: Compared to other cities, paid parking in Terneuzen's city center is very reasonable. The policy for free parking during events and free parking during elections is very good and deserves a nice compliment to this municipality.

124347.explanation: At the moment, there is little indication of insufficient parking space in the neighborhoods. The city center is an exception; it is a unique area where the best choices should be made. Do you want a cozy center that is also a safe walking area?

124348.explanation: The SP wants a car-free city center in the long term. This does not have to happen in the next four years.

124349.explanation: With warm summers, a swimming pool is not a luxury.

124351.explanation: It is very good for the municipality to invest in the future of education. School boards and parent committees should be involved more seriously in the process.

124352.explanation: These places must be properly arranged for this purpose. At present, there is too little enforcement in places where dogs often run loose where they are not allowed. Beautiful special off-leash areas could offer a solution.

124353.explanation: Absolutely not! Nuclear energy is too expensive, not clean, and arrives too late. The energy transition is necessary. It is certain that at least 90% will come from solar and wind. For the remaining 10%, powerful, modern, clean batteries (per neighborhood, per street) are the solution. Hands off the Paulinapolder.

124295.explanation: The opinion of the residents is important.

124296.explanation: Promoting resident participation and self-management enhances social cohesion.

124298.explanation: The protection of the cultural-historical landscape is paramount.

124302.explanation: Public green spaces must not be reduced.

124305.explanation: This contributes to community spirit and safety.

124308.explanation: It is important that everyone can participate in social life.

124309.explanation: The protection of the cultural-historical landscape is paramount.

124310.explanation: Occasional financial support may be necessary.

124312.explanation: Musical education contributes to the positive development of children.

124313.explanation: Occasional financial support may be necessary.

124315.explanation: More enforcement officers can contribute to citizens' sense of security.

124316.explanation: Additional camera surveillance only if it is necessary.

124318.explanation: This stimulates the flow on the housing market.

124319.explanation: This can help improve the housing market.

124320.explanation: This may be necessary to expand the housing supply.

124323.explanation: It must be prevented that residents become socially isolated due to poverty.

124325.explanation: Sustainable energy production should be encouraged.

124329.explanation: Municipal taxes and charges must not increase further.

124330.explanation: Terneuzen must follow many other municipalities in this regard.

124334.explanation: Such an allocation may be necessary.

124336.explanation: Sometimes this is the best option.

124338.explanation: This can contribute to a cultural boost for Terneuzen.

124341.explanation: We see no reason, however, for a new coffeeshop.

124342.explanation: We strive for a balanced housing market.

124344.explanation: This can help improve the housing market.

124346.explanation: The current situation still meets requirements.

124347.explanation: For the time being, there seems to be no reason for this.

124348.explanation: For the time being, there seems to be no reason for this.

124349.explanation: The current level of sports facilities must be maintained.

124351.explanation: The success of an educational campus in Terneuzen could have a positive impact on the entire municipality.

124352.explanation: As long as owners have their dogs under control.

124353.explanation: We are critical of the construction of a nuclear power plant.

124296.explanation: It increases the involvement of our residents. The risk, however, is continuity and succession.

124298.explanation: Farmers already have it tough, and the municipality can play a meaningful role in this.

124305.explanation: There is plenty of space on the piers at Terneuzen.

124308.explanation: That is part of entrepreneurship and has become so established that it is taken for granted that people handle it themselves.

124309.explanation: There are rules for this, and you cannot simply build whatever you want in the rural area. That would cause enormous uncontrolled growth.

124312.explanation: Music lessons are used too little because they are so expensive. A subsidy can prevent them from disappearing altogether.

124313.explanation: They must first see if they can merge with others.

124315.explanation: Certainly, but it proves very difficult to fill the positions.

124319.explanation: We have just started a policy for labor migrants. This causes completely uncontrollable proliferation, which is undesirable.

124320.explanation: Sometimes a village center is 'locked down' and if it does not affect the surrounding area too much, this should be possible.

124323.explanation: This limit is already 120% of the minimum income. That is already generous.

124325.explanation: An application can be made. So as a municipality, you have to facilitate, but not decide yourself how and where.

124326.explanation: There are enough main roads where 50 km/h is responsible. The smaller streets are already 30.

124327.explanation: That choice is up to the people themselves. The only question is whether the supply meets this demand.

124329.explanation: This has already been extensively researched to see if it is possible. It turned out that the method used here is the 'fairest'.

124330.explanation: Because now it is used for many other things and not entirely for the dogs and related matters.

124334.explanation: We give no one priority, everyone is equal.

124336.explanation: We do not sacrifice good land for solar panels. We already have that policy and will continue it.

124341.explanation: We have a phase-out policy in this area, so that is already arranged.

124342.explanation: Otherwise, it remains much too difficult for youth to live independently.

124344.explanation: No, because the approach involves control or enforcement that promotes exploitation.

124346.explanation: The first two hours are already free in the parking garage.

124347.explanation: Existing neighborhoods are already equipped; you cannot add more.

124348.explanation: It is already nice enough with the green belt they are currently planting. Soon it will become an obstacle and people will stop coming because it becomes inaccessible.

124349.explanation: We maintain the swimming pools. As long as the national average distance to a public pool is less than 5 km, there is no reason to abolish them, otherwise the average distance becomes about 10 km.

124351.explanation: Nonsensical. Existing building does not meet requirements; rebuilding on the existing location is highly undesirable and this way MBO is combined with VO and VSO. A good approach regarding inclusion and efficient building with attractive partners.

124352.explanation: We have the sea dike as an off-leash area. It can't get any better.

124353.explanation: Let's wait for the research first instead of shouting prematurely.

124295.explanation: Social Terneuzen has been advocating for this for years!

124296.explanation: Under certain conditions this should be possible. It is important that the progress of maintenance for public gardens remains secured and that the management of the sports hall is in good hands.

124298.explanation: It is a value added to grant this to the farmers. It gives an upgrade to the countryside.

124302.explanation: This way we keep our municipality attractively green and healthier.

124305.explanation: The municipality must organize a central, controlled fireworks display, and also offer activities in the villages so that it is not only reserved for Terneuzen.

124308.explanation: Entrepreneurs are basically responsible themselves, but in exceptional situations the municipality may provide targeted support to ensure accessibility for everyone remains feasible.

124309.explanation: Housing construction on agricultural land must be carefully considered. Unlimited building leads to fragmentation of the countryside, pressure on infrastructure, and unequal treatment.

124310.explanation: If the municipality reimburses sports memberships for people on welfare benefits, this should also apply to creative and cultural associations. It is not fair to support only sports; everyone has the right to equal opportunities to participate.

124312.explanation: Music lessons enhance children's development and subsidies ensure that all children can participate, regardless of their parents' income.

124313.explanation: The municipality should support sports associations when necessary, but with clear conditions and not at any cost.

124315.explanation: More enforcement officers are needed for a safe living environment, with extra attention to both safety and animal welfare.

124316.explanation: More camera surveillance can help counter nuisance and crime. The municipality must also actively map and address unsafe locations.

124318.explanation: Small homes for the elderly must not mean that people end up in cramped spaces. Older people have the right to a livable home with enough space to move, room for aids, overnight care, and a safe layout. This way they can live dignified and independently.

124319.explanation: If using holiday homes as primary residences contributes to solving the housing shortage, we are positive about it. The municipality should make this possible where it can be done responsibly.

124320.explanation: New construction outside village boundaries affects the characteristic landscape. Although there is a housing shortage, we believe the municipality must carefully weigh options and protect the landscape for livability.

124325.explanation: Only if there is demonstrable need should the municipality designate extra parking spaces as charging spots for electric cars.

124326.explanation: But also enforce this speed limit. In practice, people drive faster in many places.

124327.explanation: We agree that people should be able to choose care that fits their faith, culture, or beliefs. At the same time, in practice, it can sometimes be challenging to find suitable care. Care providers can also take into account the lifestyle of clients.

124329.explanation: The property tax (ozb) does not need to be lowered, but residents should pay based on the actual value of their home, not based on more expensive houses in the area.

124330.explanation: An issue that ST has been advocating for years.

124334.explanation: Priority should not be automatic. The municipality must first look at available housing options elsewhere and not just give priority to increase the population number. A careful distribution of social rental homes is paramount.

124336.explanation: Agricultural land is meant for food production and landscape. Solar panels should be placed on roofs, business buildings, and other already-built locations. This way we protect our agriculture and first use the space we already have.

124338.explanation: A new cultural center is only justifiable if there is a demonstrable need and the renovation costs do not get out of control. First thoroughly investigate before making large investments.

124341.explanation: A new coffeeshop can create healthy competition and better supervision. Through regulation and spreading out, the supply remains clear and safe for the surroundings.

124342.explanation: Nationally, 28% is social rental housing, so 40% in Terneuzen would disrupt the balance. We must ensure a healthy mix of social rental, mid-range rental, and ownership, so everyone can live suitably without distortion in the housing market.

124344.explanation: Renting out individual rooms increases the chance of abuses, nuisance, and unsafe living situations. The municipality must strictly regulate this to protect residents and neighborhoods.

124345.explanation: A village center strengthens livability, meeting, and connection. It provides facilities and a place where residents can find each other, which is especially important in larger villages for a stronger community.

124346.explanation: Terneuzen is not an expensive municipality for parking. However, subscriptions for residents of the center should be reviewed to ensure they do not pay disproportionately.

124347.explanation: Neighborhoods must have enough parking spaces that match the demand. Don’t keep skimping, but ensure a practical and livable situation for residents.

124348.explanation: The center must remain easily accessible. A fully car-free center goes too far; a low-car zone is a better balance between livability, safety, and accessibility for residents and businesses.

124349.explanation: Five outdoor swimming pools are valuable for livability. Only if the costs rise disproportionately or visitor numbers drop sharply should the municipality reconsider. Closure should never be automatic.

124351.explanation: The costs of the education campus are rising disproportionately and there is no financial overview. The project is no longer about good school buildings or education, but about filling every square meter. First stop and critically reassess what it’s truly about. An ST issue.

124352.explanation: Dogs should be able to run off-leash in more places, but safely and without nuisance. Therefore, extra fenced dog areas are a good solution: clear, controlled, and pleasant for both dogs and neighbors. An ST issue.

124353.explanation: Fully committing to a nuclear power plant in the Paulinapolder is irresponsible. The pressure on healthcare, infrastructure, and living environment will be too great, while the region will be a construction site for years. This does not fit a healthy, balanced development of Terneuzen.

124295.explanation: We are against referenda; nuances are usually lost by only being able to vote for or against. The exchange of arguments through consultation or other forms of citizen participation seems to us to do more justice to well-considered decisions.

124296.explanation: Those are temporary solutions and are often motivated by budget cuts.

124298.explanation: You have to assess each situation individually.

124302.explanation: A good agreement within the framework of climate adaptation.

124305.explanation: In addition, the possibility should be offered to organize a New Year's Eve party in the three largest towns.

124308.explanation: We would rather advocate regulating that through a permit system.

124309.explanation: The environmental vision is leading, also for farmers.

124310.explanation: We have the participation budgets, culture and sports funds, and the Learning Money foundation.

124312.explanation: That is why we subsidize Toonbeeld.

124313.explanation: We do not exclude it, but it depends on the situation. It mainly concerns what they can do themselves but also what they need it for.

124315.explanation: We see safety primarily as a task of the police. Expanding the role of public order officers in this area undermines the police and shifts responsibilities. In a rule of law, the legitimate use of coercion and force lies with the state, carried out by a national organization: the police.

124316.explanation: No, there is enough. Privacy is also important.

124318.explanation: We note that these must be affordable and close to facilities. Clustering seems to be a desirable goal in this regard.

124319.explanation: The municipality must ensure decent housing.

124320.explanation: We are in favor of infill development.

124325.explanation: Many charging stations are located near the entrance of a building. Further rules about this need to be agreed upon.

124326.explanation: This is a good thing within residential neighborhoods; through roads may retain the 50km/h limit.

124327.explanation: To prevent every belief from developing its own subsidized care institution, we advocate broad institutions that take into account the differences in the backgrounds of care recipients.

124329.explanation: Because then we will have to cut back in other areas.

124330.explanation: We have also advocated for this in recent years but have not yet gained a majority.

124334.explanation: Prioritization must be weighed individually, which means there are situations where others must take precedence.

124336.explanation: Agricultural land is for growing crops. There are many unused roofs, facades, areas, and infrastructure that can be utilized. We apply the solar ladder.

124338.explanation: That somewhat concerns us, but iconic facilities like Porgy & Bess and De Pit would then disappear. First, let's see what is promising.

124341.explanation: As far as we know, the quality of the current coffeeshop has been substandard for years. This causes the trade to move to the streets. The coffeeshop policy is precisely to prevent this. We believe a good coffeeshop can provide a solution.

124342.explanation: It is a high target percentage, but considering the many plans to grow Terneuzen, we will need to offer more rental opportunities.

124344.explanation: We are not a student city and see that people wanting to house labor migrants are filling that gap. We have advocated tightening controls due to experienced nuisance in various neighborhoods.

124345.explanation: They already exist but could possibly receive more support to fulfill their roles properly.

124346.explanation: Paid parking is very cheap in Terneuzen. Introducing paid parking and working with a permit holder system limits parking nuisance.

124348.explanation: The center is already almost car-free.

124349.explanation: We are happy with our swimming pools and propose that youth from the municipality of Terneuzen can use them free of charge.

124351.explanation: First, to create a good plan. This means it will not be a gigantic building where students get lost. It must provide safety and, besides school activities, also offer other fun, collective but also preventive activities to reduce youth care costs.

124352.explanation: Currently, there is almost a zero tolerance policy. We want that to change.

124353.explanation: The municipality must fully commit to stopping the construction of nuclear power plants in Zeeuws Vlaanderen. Their construction will have a huge impact on livability for the next 20 years.

124295.explanation: Many decisions are made without involving the residents.

124296.explanation: Residents feel much more involved in community affairs, but we must make the necessary resources available.

124298.explanation: Many farms have too low an income, and a shop or campsite gives them more financial leeway.

124302.explanation: Yes, nature is important for our health; more greenery also enhances livability.

124305.explanation: In a central location in each town and thereby maintaining the fireworks ban.

124308.explanation: Many people with disabilities currently have no access. The UN Convention states that we must also be there for them, not only municipal buildings.

124309.explanation: Yes, especially for members of one's own family, allowing the business to be preserved.

124310.explanation: We must promote participation in sports clubs, also for those with limited means.

124312.explanation: Music lessons are offered, and if one lacks the resources, they can apply to the municipality for assistance.

124315.explanation: Residents must feel safe in their city but also in the villages.

124316.explanation: Especially in shopping centers, so that adequate action can be taken.

124318.explanation: Many elderly live in houses that are too large and want to downsize, but there is insufficient opportunity to move along.

124319.explanation: Yes, this is also a way to address waiting lists, especially for starters.

124320.explanation: If necessary for businesses and business succession, this should not be a problem.

124323.explanation: Yes, it is now 120%; increasing to 130% will bring more joy to life.

124325.explanation: Yes, but also ensure lower rates.

124326.explanation: Safety in neighborhoods must come first, especially for playing children.

124329.explanation: Maintain the current level, so no increase.

124330.explanation: Yes, it is no longer appropriate; the tax is not used for its intended purpose, it goes into general funds.

124334.explanation: No, not at the expense of our own residents.

124336.explanation: Agricultural land is for agriculture, not for solar panels.

124338.explanation: Is there demand for this? Let's investigate that first.

124342.explanation: Absolutely, prices are currently too high to help everyone get a home.

124344.explanation: Yes, that is a good start for a first-time buyer.

124345.explanation: Yes, social contacts are important for our community.

124346.explanation: The first two hours are already free, so keep it that way.

124348.explanation: More walking areas, but also ensure transport for people with disabilities.

124349.explanation: No, enforce rules; investigate whether the swimming pool in Axel can be covered so it can be used year-round.

124351.explanation: If we ensure the financing is properly arranged and stays on track, there are benefits for progression in education.

124353.explanation: Depends on preconditions and compensation for existing businesses.

124295.explanation: Democracy is rule by the people. Therefore, binding referenda. It's not okay if the will of the people is corrected.

124296.explanation: Provided there is good communication and proper supervision.

124298.explanation: Free enterprise does not deserve opposition.

124302.explanation: By now, thousands of trees have been sent to the furnace due to subsidy hunger.

124305.explanation: Now that the national government has abolished a tradition because of nuisance caused by 'youths', some compensation at the local level is desirable.

124308.explanation: A financial boost will be appreciated by entrepreneurs.

124309.explanation: Every new housing is welcome. Without needing a permit.

124313.explanation: Facilitate the accommodation, not the association.

124316.explanation: Back then, with great effort, got one camera installed on the Market. Camera surveillance helps and also serves as evidence.

124319.explanation: Help and solve the housing shortage.

124326.explanation: This is the first step towards Amsterdam or Amersfoort antics. It remains necessary to always adapt to other traffic.

124336.explanation: Food production takes precedence over solar panels.

124338.explanation: I already predicted the bankruptcy in 2003. Although the council had made construction possible, later the CDA election leaflet stated that the CDA would strive for a cinema. After a few years I asked for money for the cinema. Rejected. 'Private matter.' But not in 2003!

124342.explanation: The percentage depends on many factors. Projects that are not viable do not get off the ground.

124351.explanation: Stop method: construction must be justified.

124353.explanation: About time. I already wrote about this in Metronieuws.

statements.title: Terneuzen should fully pursue the construction of a nuclear power plant.

titleParts.text: Terneuzen moet vol inzetten op de komst van een

titleParts.glossary: A plant that produces electricity by splitting the atoms of certain substances.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The residents of Terneuzen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Terneuzen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should block the opening of a new coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Terneuzen should create a new cultural center in the former cinema on Nieuwstraat in Terneuzen.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
At least 40% of newly built homes in Terneuzen should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, currently 28% of homes are social rentals.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Terneuzen moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
All larger villages in the municipality should have their own village centre.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Terneuzen should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Dogs should be allowed to run off-leash in many more places in the municipality of Terneuzen.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: More small rental and owner-occupied homes for older people should be built in Terneuzen.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The center of Terneuzen should become car-free.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Terneuzen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must stop building an education campus in Terneuzen.

shootoutStatements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The number of outdoor swimming pools in the municipality should be reduced. There are currently five.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.
Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
